title: digitalis purpurea subsp. toletana
intro: Digitalis purpurea subsp. toletana is a subspecies of the Foxglove plant, a biennial herb native to Portugal. It is known for its medicinal properties, as it contains cardiac glycosides, specifically digoxin and digoxigenin, which have been used to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The medicinal properties of Digitalis purpurea subsp. toletana have been found to have health benefits such as improving heart function, reducing symptoms of heart failure, and lowering blood pressure. The plant is commonly prepared as an herbal tincture, infusion, or dried flower extract, which can be used to create medicinal products under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
